MEMBER.NAME: CSR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: ADC Common status register, Address offset: ADC1 base address + 0x300

MEMBER.NAME: CCR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: ADC common control register, Address offset: ADC1 base address + 0x304

MEMBER.NAME: CDR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: ADC common regular data register for dual AND triple modes, Address offset: ADC1 base address + 0x308

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_Mode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Configures the ADC to operate in independent or multi mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_Common_mode

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_Prescaler
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Select the frequency of the clock to the ADC. The clock is common for all the ADCs. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_Prescaler

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_DMAAccessMode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Configures the Direct memory access mode for multi ADC mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_Direct_memory_access_mode_for_multi_mode

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_TwoSamplingDelay
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Configures the Delay between 2 sampling phases. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_delay_between_2_sampling_phases

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_Resolution
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Configures the ADC resolution dual mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_resolution

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_ScanConvMode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the conversion is performed in Scan (multichannels) or Single (one channel) mode. This parameter can be set to ENABLE or DISABLE

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_ContinuousConvMode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the conversion is performed in Continuous or Single mode. This parameter can be set to ENABLE or DISABLE.

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_ExternalTrigConvEdge
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Select the external trigger edge and enable the trigger of a regular group. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_external_trigger_edge_for_regular_channels_conversion

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_ExternalTrigConv
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Select the external event used to trigger the start of conversion of a regular group. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_extrenal_trigger_sources_for_regular_channels_conversion

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_DataAlign
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the ADC data alignment is left or right. This parameter can be a value of @ref ADC_data_align

MEMBER.NAME: ADC_NbrOfConversion
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the number of ADC conversions that will be done using the sequencer for regular channel group. This parameter must range from 1 to 16.

MEMBER.NAME: DAC_Trigger
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the external trigger for the selected DAC channel. This parameter can be a value of @ref DAC_trigger_selection

MEMBER.NAME: DAC_WaveGeneration
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether DAC channel noise waves or triangle waves are generated, or whether no wave is generated. This parameter can be a value of @ref DAC_wave_generation

MEMBER.NAME: DAC_LFSRUnmask_TriangleAmplitude
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the LFSR mask for noise wave generation or the maximum amplitude triangle generation for the DAC channel. This parameter can be a value of @ref DAC_lfsrunmask_triangleamplitude

MEMBER.NAME: DAC_OutputBuffer
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the DAC channel output buffer is enabled or disabled. This parameter can be a value of @ref DAC_output_buffer

MEMBER.NAME: NVIC_IRQChannel
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the IRQ channel to be enabled or disabled. This parameter can be an enumerator of @ref IRQn_Type enumeration (For the complete STM32 Devices IRQ Channels list, please refer to stm32f4xx.h file)

MEMBER.NAME: NVIC_IRQChannelPreemptionPriority
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the pre-emption priority for the IRQ channel specified in NVIC_IRQChannel. This parameter can be a value between 0 and 15 as described in the table @ref MISC_NVIC_Priority_Table A lower priority value indicates a higher priority

MEMBER.NAME: NVIC_IRQChannelSubPriority
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the subpriority level for the IRQ channel specified in NVIC_IRQChannel. This parameter can be a value between 0 and 15 as described in the table @ref MISC_NVIC_Priority_Table A lower priority value indicates a higher priority

MEMBER.NAME: NVIC_IRQChannelCmd
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the IRQ channel defined in NVIC_IRQChannel will be enabled or disabled. This parameter can be set either to ENABLE or DISABLE

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_Direction
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the SPI unidirectional or bidirectional data mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_data_direction

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_Mode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the SPI operating mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_mode

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_DataSize
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the SPI data size. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_data_size

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_CPOL
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the serial clock steady state. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_Clock_Polarity

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_CPHA
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the clock active edge for the bit capture. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_Clock_Phase

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_NSS
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the NSS signal is managed by hardware (NSS pin) or by software using the SSI bit. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_Slave_Select_management

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_BaudRatePrescaler
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the Baud Rate prescaler value which will be used to configure the transmit and receive SCK clock. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_BaudRate_Prescaler @note The communication clock is derived from the master clock. The slave clock does not need to be set.

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_FirstBit
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether data transfers start from MSB or LSB bit. This parameter can be a value of @ref SPI_MSB_LSB_transmission

MEMBER.NAME: SPI_CRCPolynomial
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the polynomial used for the CRC calculation.

MEMBER.NAME: TIM_Prescaler
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the prescaler value used to divide the TIM clock. This parameter can be a number between 0x0000 and 0xFFFF

MEMBER.NAME: TIM_CounterMode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the counter mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref TIM_Counter_Mode

MEMBER.NAME: TIM_Period
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the period value to be loaded into the active Auto-Reload Register at the next update event. This parameter must be a number between 0x0000 and 0xFFFF.

MEMBER.NAME: TIM_ClockDivision
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the clock division. This parameter can be a value of @ref TIM_Clock_Division_CKD

MEMBER.NAME: TIM_RepetitionCounter
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the repetition counter value. Each time the RCR downcounter reaches zero, an update event is generated and counting restarts from the RCR value (N). This means in PWM mode that (N+1) corresponds to: - the number of PWM periods in edge-aligned mode - the number of half PWM period in center-aligned mode This parameter must be a number between 0x00 and 0xFF. @note This parameter is valid only for TIM1 and TIM8.

MEMBER.NAME: USART_BaudRate
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: This member configures the USART communication baud rate. The baud rate is computed using the following formula: - IntegerDivider = ((PCLKx) / (8 * (OVR8+1) * (USART_InitStruct->USART_BaudRate))) - FractionalDivider = ((IntegerDivider - ((u32) IntegerDivider)) * 8 * (OVR8+1)) + 0.5 Where OVR8 is the "oversampling by 8 mode" configuration bit in the CR1 register.

MEMBER.NAME: USART_WordLength
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the number of data bits transmitted or received in a frame. This parameter can be a value of @ref USART_Word_Length

MEMBER.NAME: USART_StopBits
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the number of stop bits transmitted. This parameter can be a value of @ref USART_Stop_Bits

MEMBER.NAME: USART_Parity
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies the parity mode. This parameter can be a value of @ref USART_Parity @note When parity is enabled, the computed parity is inserted at the MSB position of the transmitted data (9th bit when the word length is set to 9 data bits; 8th bit when the word length is set to 8 data bits).

MEMBER.NAME: USART_Mode
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies whether the Receive or Transmit mode is enabled or disabled. This parameter can be a value of @ref USART_Mode

MEMBER.NAME: USART_HardwareFlowControl
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Specifies wether the hardware flow control mode is enabled or disabled. This parameter can be a value of @ref USART_Hardware_Flow_Control

MEMBER.NAME: SR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Status register, Address offset: 0x00

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED0
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x02

MEMBER.NAME: DR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Data register, Address offset: 0x04

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED1
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x06

MEMBER.NAME: BRR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Baud rate register, Address offset: 0x08

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED2
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x0A

MEMBER.NAME: CR1
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Control register 1, Address offset: 0x0C

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED3
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x0E

MEMBER.NAME: CR2
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Control register 2, Address offset: 0x10

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED4
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x12

MEMBER.NAME: CR3
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Control register 3, Address offset: 0x14

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED5
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x16

MEMBER.NAME: GTPR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: USART Guard time and prescaler register, Address offset: 0x18

MEMBER.NAME: RESERVED6
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: Reserved, 0x1A

MEMBER.NAME: CR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: WWDG Control register, Address offset: 0x00

MEMBER.NAME: CFR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: WWDG Configuration register, Address offset: 0x04

MEMBER.NAME: SR
MEMBER.REGULAR_CMT: WWDG Status register, Address offset: 0x08

Name: main Address: 0x08032960 Full hash/reference: 0xa4a9247ef6c832b4 Specific hash: 0xa069d7dd2649ced6 Suffix: f Source file: startup_stm32f427xx.s Notes: Nu: Initializes stuff. Then prints out current firmware version info. Then checks battery. Then does TIM2 and I2C2 stuff. I believe the 'G-Core' stuff might happen inside FUN_0802afa4 while the STM32 waits in a loop at 08032a6a. If the loop exits at 08032a76 then the STM32 resets itself: GUI_GetBkColor disableTIM2CR1-2_TIM3_TIM4_APB1PeriphClocks disableDMA2S6CR_DMA2S7CR_USART1DRnCR1-3_USART6DRnCR1-3_APB2PeriphClockCmd saving something to flashmem and then disabling GPIOG.ODR0&1 GPIOE.ODR7&8 GPIOG.ODR11 GPIOE.ODR9&14 GPIOD.ODR4 GPIOF.ODR11 GPIOD.ODR3&2 GPIOE.ODR11 then it waits for GPIO.IDRs to be empty then turns off GPIOE.ODR11 again and then finally force-resets itself by sending VECTKEY with SYS RESET REQ to SCB.AIRCR.

FUNCTION.NAME: main
FUNCTION.REGULAR_CMT: Name: main Address: 0x08032960 Full hash/reference: 0xa4a9247ef6c832b4 Specific hash: 0xa069d7dd2649ced6 Suffix: f Source file: startup_stm32f427xx.s Notes: Nu: Initializes stuff. Then prints out current firmware version info. Then checks battery. Then does TIM2 and I2C2 stuff. I believe the 'G-Core' stuff might happen inside FUN_0802afa4 while the STM32 waits in a loop at 08032a6a. If the loop exits at 08032a76 then the STM32 resets itself: GUI_GetBkColor disableTIM2CR1-2_TIM3_TIM4_APB1PeriphClocks disableDMA2S6CR_DMA2S7CR_USART1DRnCR1-3_USART6DRnCR1-3_APB2PeriphClockCmd saving something to flashmem and then disabling GPIOG.ODR0&1 GPIOE.ODR7&8 GPIOG.ODR11 GPIOE.ODR9&14 GPIOD.ODR4 GPIOF.ODR11 GPIOD.ODR3&2 GPIOE.ODR11 then it waits for GPIO.IDRs to be empty then turns off GPIOE.ODR11 again and then finally force-resets itself by sending VECTKEY with SYS RESET REQ to SCB.AIRCR.

FUNCTION.NAME: SystemInit
FUNCTION.REGULAR_CMT: Name: SystemInit Address: 0x08032bf8 Full hash/reference: 0xd39fe65cf775cbc6 Specific hash: 0xf87cf0819a4d4054 Suffix: f Gdt: STM32F427_437xx Source library: STM32F4xx_DSP_StdPeriph_Lib_V1.9.0 Source file: startup_stm32f427xx.s / system_stm32f4xx.c Notes: Turns on FPU 11 & 10 and sets system clock to HSI (instead of HSE) [pm0214 p253 & rm0090 p161-7].
